title: Shadai Farm

infobox.value: (社台スタリオンステーション, *Shadai Sutarion Sutēshon*) is a thoroughbred breeding facility located in [Abira](/wiki/Abira,_Hokkaid%C5%8D "Abira, Hokkaidō") on the island of [Hokkaido](/wiki/Hokkaido "Hokkaido") in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"). The farm was begun in the late 1970s to early 1980s by the late Zenya Yoshida, and is now run jointly by his sons (Katsumi, Haruya, and Teruya Yoshida), known collectively as the Shadai Group.[[1]](#cite_note-racing-1) As of 2006, the brothers own 3,000 horses worldwide.[[1]](#cite_note-racing-1) The farm houses stallions from Japan and many other countries and racing circuits.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The grounds also house a racing museum and tourist park called the Northern Horse Park and the Northern Farm Kuko, a large horse-training and conditioning facility. The most expensive acquisition was [War Emblem](/wiki/War_Emblem "War Emblem"), which was bought for US$17 million in 2002 to replace [Sunday Silence](/wiki/Sunday_Silence "Sunday Silence"), which died that year of laminitis (and whose offspring are most notable among the stable's horses today). War Emblem was a reluctant breeder, siring only 106 registered foals between 2004 and 2011.[[3]](#cite_note-macdonald-3) He did not sire a foal after 2012, and was pensioned and returned to the United States in the fall of 2015 to [Old Friends Equine](/wiki/Old_Friends_Equine "Old Friends Equine").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Other recent acquisitions include 2010 [King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes](/wiki/King_George_VI_and_Queen_Elizabeth_Stakes "King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes") winner [Harbinger](/wiki/Harbinger_(horse) "Harbinger (horse)")[[5]](#cite_note-stud-5) and 2010 [Epsom Derby](/wiki/2010_Epsom_Derby "2010 Epsom Derby") and [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe") winner [Workforce](/wiki/Workforce_(horse) "Workforce (horse)").[[6]](#cite_note-thoroughbredtimes1-6)

Angst, Frank (July 21, 2015). ["War Emblem to Join Old Friends in September"](http://www.bloodhorse.com/horse-racing/articles/93112/war-emblem-to-join-old-friends-in-september). *Blood-Horse*. Blood-Horse LLC. Retrieved 8 November 2015.

["European champion Workforce to stand in Japan at Shadai"](https://web.archive.org/web/20111116073040/http://www.thoroughbredtimes.com/breeding-news/2011/11/14/workforce-retired-to-stand-in-japan-at-shadai.aspx). Thoroughbred Times. 2011-11-14. Archived from [the original](http://www.thoroughbredtimes.com/breeding-news/2011/11/14/workforce-retired-to-stand-in-japan-at-shadai.aspx) on 2011-11-16. Retrieved 2012-06-15.
["2026 stud fees for stallions at Shadai Stallion Station announced [Updated 12/18]"](https://shadai-ss.com/info/7569/). *Shadai Stallion Station*. Retrieved 2025-12-23.
["社台スタリオンステーションにエフフォーリアがスタッドイン | 馬産地ニュース | 競走馬のふるさと案内所"](https://uma-furusato.com/news/entry-61501.html). *uma-fur

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [42°42′57″N 141°49′26″E﻿ / ﻿42.71583°N 141.82389°E﻿ / 42.71583; 141.82389](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Shadai_Stallion_Station&params=42_42_57_N_141_49_26_E_region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Shadai Farm](/w/index.php?title=Shadai_Farm&redirect=no "Shadai Farm"))

Thoroughbred breeding facility in Abira, Hokkaidō, Japan

[![Map](https://maps.wikimedia.org/img/osm-intl,11,42.71583333,141.82388889,300x200.png?lang=en&domain=en.wikipedia.org&title=Shadai_Stallion_Station&revid=1337913054&groups=_5873a8373adffc4b0b18d9df22dcf60b7a69e70b&parser=legacy)](/wiki/Special:Map/11/42.71583333/141.82388889/en)

Map

**Shadai Stallion Station** (社台スタリオンステーション, *Shadai Sutarion Sutēshon*) is a thoroughbred breeding facility located in [Abira](/wiki/Abira,_Hokkaid%C5%8D "Abira, Hokkaidō") on the island of [Hokkaido](/wiki/Hokkaido "Hokkaido") in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"). The farm was begun in the late 1970s to early 1980s by the late Zenya Yoshida, and is now run jointly by his sons (Katsumi, Haruya, and Teruya Yoshida), known collectively as the Shadai Group.[[1]](#cite_note-racing-1) As of 2006, the brothers own 3,000 horses worldwide.[[1]](#cite_note-racing-1) The farm houses stallions from Japan and many other countries and racing circuits.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The grounds also house a racing museum and tourist park called the Northern Horse Park and the Northern Farm Kuko, a large horse-training and conditioning facility. The most expensive acquisition was [War Emblem](/wiki/War_Emblem "War Emblem"), which was bought for US$17 million in 2002 to replace [Sunday Silence](/wiki/Sunday_Silence "Sunday Silence"), which died that year of laminitis (and whose offspring are most notable among the stable's horses today). War Emblem was a reluctant breeder, siring only 106 registered foals between 2004 and 2011.[[3]](#cite_note-macdonald-3) He did not sire a foal after 2012, and was pensioned and returned to the United States in the fall of 2015 to [Old Friends Equine](/wiki/Old_Friends_Equine "Old Friends Equine").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Other recent acquisitions include 2010 [King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes](/wiki/King_George_VI_and_Queen_Elizabeth_Stakes "King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes") winner [Harbinger](/wiki/Harbinger_(horse) "Harbinger (horse)")[[5]](#cite_note-stud-5) and 2010 [Epsom Derby](/wiki/2010_Epsom_Derby "2010 Epsom Derby") and [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe") winner [Workforce](/wiki/Workforce_(horse) "Workforce (horse)").[[6]](#cite_note-thoroughbredtimes1-6)

## Current stallions

## Cemetery

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shadai_Stallion_Station&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cemetery")]

The graves of several past stallions, such as Northern Taste, Sunday Silence, Deep Impact, and King Kamehameha, are located on the hills within the property.[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)
